2605.05118.title: On the Wasserstein Gradient Flow Interpretation of Drifting Models
2605.05118.paper_summary: Recently, Deng et al. (2026) proposed Generative Modeling via Drifting (GMD), a novel framework for generative tasks. This note presents an analysis of GMD through the lens of Wasserstein Gradient Flows (WGF), i.e., the path of steepest descent for a functional in the space of probability measures, equipped with the geometry of optimal transport. Unlike previous WGF-based contributions, GMD can be thought of as directly targeting a fixed point of a specific WGF flow. We demonstrate three main results: first, that one algorithm proposed by Deng et al. (2026) corresponds to finding the limiting point of a WGF on the KL divergence, with Parzen smoothing on the densities. Second, that the algorithm actually implemented by Deng et al. (2026) corresponds to a different procedure, which bears some resemblance to the fixed point of a WGF on the Sinkhorn divergence, but lacks certain desirable properties of the latter. Third, the same same idea can be extended to the limiting point of other WGFs, including the Maximum Mean Discrepancy (MMD), the sliced Wasserstein distance, and GAN critic functions.
2605.05118.paper_summary_zh: 最近，鄧等人（2026）提出了一種名為生成建模漂移（Generative Modeling via Drifting, GMD）的新框架，用於生成任務。本文通過瓦瑟斯坦梯度流（Wasserstein Gradient Flows, WGF）的視角對GMD進行分析，即在概率測度空間中，配備最佳運輸幾何的函數的最陡下降路徑。與之前基於WGF的貢獻不同，GMD可以被視為直接針對特定WGF流的固定點。我們展示了三個主要結果：首先，鄧等人（2026）提出的某個算法對應於在KL散度上找到WGF的極限點，並對密度進行Parzen平滑。其次，鄧等人（2026）實際實施的算法對應於一個不同的程序，該程序與WGF在Sinkhorn散度上的固定點有些相似，但缺乏後者某些理想的特性。第三，這一相同的思路可以擴展到其他WGF的極限點，包括最大均值差異（Maximum Mean Discrepancy, MMD）、切片瓦瑟斯坦距離和GAN評估函數。
2605.05118.author: Arthur Gretton et.al.
2605.05118.authors: Arthur Gretton, Li Kevin Wenliang, Alexandre Galashov, James Thornton, Valentin De Bortoli, Arnaud Doucet
